revision.comment: 新しいページ：東国六腹朝臣。
revision.text:
'''東国六腹朝臣'''（あづまむつはらのあそみ）は、[[毛野]]地域出身で出自を同じくすると称する、以下の6[[氏族]]の総称。
* [[上毛野氏|上毛野朝臣]] * [[下毛野氏|下毛野朝臣]] * 大野朝臣 * 池田朝臣 * 佐味朝臣 * 車持朝臣
== 文献上の記載 == === 国史 === 『[[日本書紀]]』[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）11月条では、[[八色の姓]]において「[[朝臣]]」の姓を授けられた52氏の中に、上毛野君、車持君、下毛野君、佐味君、大野君、池田君の6氏の名が見える。
『[[続日本紀]]』[[延暦]]10年（[[791年]]）4月条では、池原公綱主らの言の中に、池原・上毛野2氏の出自が[[豊城入彦命]]であること、豊城入彦命の子孫である「東国六腹朝臣」は居地によって姓と氏を得たことが記載されている。
また『[[日本三代実録]]』[[元慶]]元年（[[877年]]）12月25日条では、上毛野、大野、池田、佐味、車持朝臣が[[崇神天皇]]の後裔として同祖だと記載されている。
=== 新撰姓氏録 === [[平安時代]]初期の[[弘仁]]6年（[[815年]]）『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、いずれも皇別の同族として以下のように記載されている。 * 右京 上毛野朝臣 - 崇神天皇皇子・豊城入彦命の後<ref>なお左京にも上毛野朝臣の名が見えるが、田辺史系の上毛野氏であり、東国六腹とは異なる。</ref> * 左京 下毛野朝臣 - 崇神天皇皇子・豊城入彦命の後 * 右京 大野朝臣 - 豊城入彦命四世孫・[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]の後 * 左京 池田朝臣 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命十世孫・佐太公の後 * 右京 佐味朝臣 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命の後 * 左京 車持公 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命八世孫・射狭君の後
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:あつまむつはらのあそみ}} [[Category:毛野氏|**]]

'''東国六腹朝臣'''（あづまのくにむつはらのあそみ）は、[[毛野]]地域出身で出自を同じくすると称する、以下の6[[氏族]]の総称。

また『[[日本三代実録]]』[[元慶]]元年（[[877年]]）12月25日条では、上毛野、大野、池田、佐味、車持朝臣が[[崇神天皇]]の後裔として同祖だと記載されている。
=== 新撰姓氏録 === [[平安時代]]初期の[[弘仁]]6年（[[815年]]）『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、いずれも皇別の同族として以下のように記載されている。 * 右京 上毛野朝臣 - 崇神天皇皇子・豊城入彦命の後<ref>なお左京にも上毛野朝臣の名が見えるが、田辺史系の上毛野氏であり、東国六腹とは異なる。</ref> * 左京 下毛野朝臣 - 崇神天皇皇子・豊城入彦命の後 * 右京 大野朝臣 - 豊城入彦命四世孫・[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]の後 * 左京 池田朝臣 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命十世孫・佐太公の後 * 右京 佐味朝臣 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命の後 * 左京 車持公 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命八世孫・射狭君の後
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:あつまのくにむつはらのあそみ}} [[Category:毛野氏|**]]

revision.text:
'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

[[Category:bump]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[ja:東国六腹朝臣]]

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].

* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces]][[Category:qq]]

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}

The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== Related Topics ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq deprecated close]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.

=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}

The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
