== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

revision.text:
'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|新撰姓氏録" to "|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku"
revision.text:
'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the [[Eight Prince Ranks]] (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno region|Keno]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno Province]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno Province]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno Province]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno Province]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

revision.text:
'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno Province]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno Province]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno Province]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno Province]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno Province]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}} (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣|12=draft}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣|12=draft}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣|12=draft}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|12=draft}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣|12=draft}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣|12=draft}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公|12=draft}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[Keno Province]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）|12=draft}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the rank of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft}} (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[Heian period]], the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣|12=draft}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣|12=draft}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣|12=draft}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|12=draft}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣|12=draft}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣|12=draft}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公|12=draft}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}}

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
Text replacement - "|豊城入彦命|" to "|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|"
5 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:東国六腹朝臣 up to rev 53028078
revision.text:
'''東国六腹朝臣'''（あづまのくにむつはらのあそみ）は、[[毛野]]地域出身で出自を同じくすると称する、次の6[[氏族]]の総称。
* [[上毛野氏|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）]] * [[下毛野氏|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）]] * 大野朝臣（大野氏） * 池田朝臣（池田氏） * 佐味朝臣（佐味氏） * 車持朝臣（車持氏）
== 記録 == === 国史 === 『[[日本書紀]]』[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）11月条では、[[八色の姓]]において「[[朝臣]]」の姓を授けられた52氏の中に、上毛野君、車持君、下毛野君、佐味君、大野君、池田君の6氏の名が見える。
『[[続日本紀]]』[[延暦]]10年（[[791年]]）4月条では、池原公綱主らの言の中に、池原・上毛野2氏の出自が[[豊城入彦命]]であること、豊城入彦命の子孫である「東国六腹朝臣」は居地によって姓と氏を得たことが記載されている。
また『[[日本三代実録]]』[[元慶]]元年（[[877年]]）12月25日条では、上毛野、大野、池田、佐味、車持朝臣が[[崇神天皇]]の後裔として同祖だと記載されている。
=== 新撰姓氏録 === [[平安時代]]初期の[[弘仁]]6年（[[815年]]）『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、いずれも皇別の同族として次のように記載されている。 * 右京 上毛野朝臣 - 崇神天皇皇子の豊城入彦命の後<ref>なお左京にも上毛野朝臣の名が見えるが、田辺史系の上毛野氏であり、東国六腹とは異なる。</ref> * 左京 下毛野朝臣 - 崇神天皇皇子の豊城入彦命の後 * 右京 大野朝臣 - 豊城入彦命四世孫の[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]の後 * 左京 池田朝臣 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命十世孫の佐太公の後 * 右京 佐味朝臣 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命の後 * 左京 車持公 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命八世孫の射狭君の後
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[上毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:あつまのくにむつはらのあそみ}} [[Category:上毛野氏|**]]

『[[続日本紀]]』[[延暦]]10年（[[791年]]）4月条では、池原公綱主らの言の中に、池原・上毛野2氏の出自が[[豊城入彦命]]であること、豊城入彦命の子孫である「東国六腹朝臣」は居地によって姓と氏を得たことが記載されている。
また『[[日本三代実録]]』[[元慶]]元年（[[877年]]）12月25日条では、上毛野、大野、池田、佐味、車持朝臣が[[崇神天皇]]の後裔として同祖だと記載されている。
=== 新撰姓氏録 === [[平安時代]]初期の[[弘仁]]6年（[[815年]]）『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、いずれも皇別の同族として次のように記載されている。 * 右京 上毛野朝臣 - 崇神天皇皇子の豊城入彦命の後<ref>なお左京にも上毛野朝臣の名が見えるが、田辺史系の上毛野氏であり、東国六腹とは異なる。</ref> * 左京 下毛野朝臣 - 崇神天皇皇子の豊城入彦命の後 * 右京 大野朝臣 - 豊城入彦命四世孫の[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]の後 * 左京 池田朝臣 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命十世孫の佐太公の後 * 右京 佐味朝臣 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命の後 * 左京 車持公 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命八世孫の射狭君の後
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[上毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:あつまのくにむつはらのあそみ}} [[Category:上毛野氏|**]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q16142695]]
Wikidata name: Azuma no Kuni Mutsuhara no Asomi
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] area:

* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Records ==

=== Historical Chronicles ===
The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the Rank of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} (八色の姓).

The ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.

The ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].

=== Shinsen Shōjiroku ===
In the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage:
* {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref>
* {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin
* {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Ōno no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Sami no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

== References ==
{{reflist}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q16142695]]
Wikidata name: Azuma no Kuni Mutsuhara no Asomi
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] area:

* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Records ==

=== Historical Chronicles ===
The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the Rank of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} (八色の姓).

The ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.

The ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].

=== Shinsen Shōjiroku ===
In the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage:
* {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref>
* {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin
* {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

=== Historical Chronicles ===
The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the Rank of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} (八色の姓).

The ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.

The ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].

=== Shinsen Shōjiroku ===
In the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage:
* {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref>
* {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin
* {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

* {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
