{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q16142695]]
Wikidata name: Azuma no Kuni Mutsuhara no Asomi
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] area:

* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Records ==

=== Historical Chronicles ===
The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the Rank of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} (八色の姓).

The ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.

The ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].

=== Shinsen Shōjiroku ===
In the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage:
* {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref>
* {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin
* {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q16142695]]
Wikidata name: Azuma no Kuni Mutsuhara no Asomi
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] area:

* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Records ==

=== Historical Chronicles ===
The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the Rank of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} (八色の姓).

The ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.

The ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].

=== Shinsen Shōjiroku ===
In the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage:
* {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref>
* {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin
* {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
* {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the Rank of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} (八色の姓).
The ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

=== Historical Chronicles === The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the Rank of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} (八色の姓).
The ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. The ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].

=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

{{wikidata link|Q16142695}} [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kamitsukeno clan]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q16142695]] Wikidata name: Azuma no Kuni Mutsuhara no Asomi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the Rank of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} (八色の姓).
The ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q16142695}} [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kamitsukeno clan]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:qq]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q16142695]] Wikidata name: Azuma no Kuni Mutsuhara no Asomi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', in its entry for the 13th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the Rank of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} (八色の姓).
The ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'', in its entry for the 10th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', in its entry for the 1st year of [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|東国六腹朝臣|version=53028078|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q16142695]] Wikidata name: Azuma no Kuni Mutsuhara no Asomi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the {{ill|Keno Province|en|Keno Province|WD=Q1886428|lt=Keno Province}} area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the Rank of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}} under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku}} (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō}} (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}.
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|東国六腹朝臣|version=53028078|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]]
'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the {{ill|Keno Province|en|Keno Province|WD=Q1886428|lt=Keno Province}} area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the Rank of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}} under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku}} (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō}} (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}.
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|東国六腹朝臣|version=53028078|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]]

* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Records ==
=== Historical Chronicles === The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', in its entry for the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} (684), lists these six clans—Kamitsukeno no Kimi, Kuramochi no Kimi, Shimotsukeno no Kimi, Sami no Kimi, Ōno no Kimi, and Ikeda no Kimi—among the 52 clans granted the Rank of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason|1=Ason}} under the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} (八色の姓).
The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}. It further explains that the descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, referred to as the "Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason," were granted their surnames and clan names based on their residences.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in its entry for the 1st year of {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō|1=Gangyō}} (877), mentions that the Kamitsukeno, Ōno, Ikeda, Sami, and Kuramochi clans all share a common ancestry as descendants of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}.
=== Shinsen Shōjiroku === In the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (compiled in 815) lists the following clans as part of the same imperial lineage: * {{ill|Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja|右京 上毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Kamitsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}<ref>There is also a Kamitsukeno no Ason listed under the Left Capital, but this belongs to the Tanabe-no-Fumi lineage and is unrelated to the Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara.</ref> * {{ill|Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja|左京 下毛野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Shimotsukeno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto, the son of Emperor Sujin * {{ill|Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja|右京 大野朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Ōno no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of {{ill|Aratawake|ja|大荒田別命|Aratawake=Aratawake|lt=Ōaratawake-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別}}, the fourth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja|左京 池田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Ikeda no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Sada-kimi, the tenth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja|右京 佐味朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Capital Sami no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto * {{ill|Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja|左京 車持公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Capital Kuramochi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Same lineage as Kamitsukeno no Ason; descendants of Isasa-kimi, the eighth-generation descendant of Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東国六腹朝臣]]
'''Azuma no Kuni Mutsu Hara no Ason''' (東国六腹朝臣) refers to a collective term for the following six clans, all of which claim common origins in the {{ill|Keno Province|en|Keno Province|WD=Q1886428|lt=Keno Province|1=Keno Province}} area:
* {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja|上毛野朝臣（上毛野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Ason (Kamitsukeno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野朝臣（下毛野氏）|Shimotsukeno clan=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno no Ason (Shimotsukeno clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja|大野朝臣（大野氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Ason (Ōno clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja|池田朝臣（池田氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Ason (Ikeda clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja|佐味朝臣（佐味氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Ason (Sami clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja|車持朝臣（車持氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi no Ason (Kuramochi clan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'', in its entry for the 10th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} (791), states that the Ikebara clan and Kamitsukeno clan traced their lineage to {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}.
